description: Letter in Judaeo-Arabic (right side of recto and verso) from [Ya'qu?]b b. Bū l-Faraj, probably in Alexandria, to Abū l-M[unā?] al-[ʿAṭṭār?], probably in Fustat. The writer is in difficult straits and has nothing left to sell except some drugs (ʿaqāqir), and requests the recipient's help. He also mentions the son of the deaf man (Ibn al-Ḥeresh Abū Ib[rahim?]) and Abū l-Makārim b. al-Amshāṭī. ASE.
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מ-[יעקו]ב בן אבו אל-פרג', ככל הנראה באלכסנדריה, לאבו אל-מ[ונא?] אל-[עטאר?] בפסטאט; הכותב במצוקה קשה ומבקש עזרה
